question:
As a teenager, the sight of Mount Kilimanjaro rising majestically above the Serengeti made Vern Jones desperately want to climb it. Finally in 2014, at the age of 62, Vern finally realised his dream while accompanied by his daughters and their husbands. However, he was all too aware that he couldn't have succeeded without the help of the guides.

Why did Vern Jones choose to go to Mount Kilimanjaro?
choices: He felt it would be a good opportunity to start a business.
He wanted to join his family who were planning to go there.
He'd been inspired by images of the area from a young age.
He wanted to learn how to help other people climb it.

question:
Vern's response was to found Kili Summit Club which raises money to help the guides from the proceeds of mountain gear sales. They receive footwear and equipment to help them with their work, as well as commission from sales.

What difference has Kili Summit Club made?
choices: It helps guides find well-paid work in other areas.
It raises money and provides resources for guides.
It reduces the risk of accidents for guides.
It ensures guides receive a higher salary than before.

question:
Integral to Lisa's business philosophy is making Sister Pie a part of the community. Due to this, she operates a 'Pie it Forward' system where customers can purchase a coupon.

What is mentioned as being important for Lisa about her business?
choices: It needs to be a place where all local people feel welcome.
It should raise as much money as possible for charities.
It has to be successful enough to open more bakeries in the area.
It must become the most famous place in Detroit for pies.

question:
They told her that the man, who wished to remain anonymous, wanted to donate $10,000 to the foundation in her name.

The word 'anonymous' means:
choices: referring to someone whose name is not made public
someone who works for a charity

question:
In 2015, Tracy Warshal was in a queue at a busy shop when the man in front of her forgot his wallet. Tracy paid for the stranger's milk and other items. A month later she was contacted by colleagues at the cancer charity where she worked. They told her that the man wanted to donate $10,000 to the foundation in her name.

What was the result of Tracy's action of paying for the stranger?
choices: Tracy was given a job with more money and responsibility.
People suffering from illness were able to receive additional help.
There was an increase in the number of donations to her charity.
Tracy was able to meet the stranger and thank him for his help.

question: The word which means 'being busy or working very hard' is:
She even did a 24-hour fund-raising activity, bringing in $26,593, which allowed her to open Sister Pie in April, 2015. The word 'proceeds' means:
choices: money paid to a salesperson for selling something
the total money received from selling or organising something
an organisation started for a specific purpose
a person who does a job to get experience

question: The word which means 'not having the correct tools or skills to do something' is:
Use the words to complete the phrase: ________ fresh water supplies.
Use the words to complete the phrase: ________ water filters.
Use the words to complete the phrase: ________ new toilets.
The Jordanian Royal Family helps to improve health ________.
The Jordanian Royal Family aims to give better work ________ to women and young people.
The Jordanian Royal Family promotes sustainable development that does not harm the ________.
Complete the word building table. The noun form of the verb 'enforce' is:
Complete the word building table. The verb form of the noun 'discrimination' is:
Complete the word building table. The noun form of the verb 'justify' is:
Monkeys live ________ fruit and nuts.
Monkeys live ________ trees.
We eat soup ________ of a bowl.
We need to deal ________ the climate crisis.

The reporter said that in today's programme, they would look at some of the amazing things that different charities were doing.

What did the reporter say originally in direct speech?
choices: 'In today's programme, we will look at some of the amazing things that different charities are doing.'
'In today's programme, we looked at some of the amazing things that different charities were doing.'
'In today's programme, we had looked at some of the amazing things that different charities had been doing.'
'In today's programme, we were looking at some of the amazing things that different charities did.'

question:
The professor told us to remember 90% of the world's natural disasters were related to water.

What did the professor say originally in direct speech?
choices: 'You should remember 90% of the world's natural disasters are related to water.'
'Remember, 90% of the world's natural disasters are related to water.'
'We remember 90% of the world's natural disasters are related to water.'
'Remembered that 90% of the world's natural disasters are related to water.'

question:
'Ali has left early.'
Sami said that ________.

'She must leave.'
Hani said that ________.

'I will buy a car.' (speaker is male)
Huda said that ________.

'Can you speak French?'
Sami asked them ________.

'Are you going to the party tonight?'
She asked me ________.
choices: if I was going to the party that night.
was I going to the party tonight.
if was I going to the party tonight.
I was going to the party tonight.

question:
'Don't forget your umbrella.'
He told me ________.

'Will you pass me the salt?'
He asked her ________.
choices: to will pass him the salt.

question:
Abeer: 'It was that man who stole the money!'
Abeer ________.
choices: agreed a man of stealing the money.
accused a man of stealing the money.
refused a man of stealing the money.
admitted a man of stealing the money.

question:
Adel: 'I didn't break Jamal's phone!'
Adel ________.

Habib: 'I'll help you with the boxes.'
Habib ________.
choices: refused to help with the boxes.
offered to help with the boxes.
denied to help with the boxes.
insisted on help with the boxes.

question:
Reem: 'I'm really sorry I forgot your birthday, Suha.'
Reem ________.
The university where he worked admitted that ________ (make) him leave before he was 69.
Which phrase expresses a STRONG opinion?
choices: It's a good idea in principle, but ...

question: Which phrase expresses a TENTATIVE opinion?
choices: Without a shadow of a doubt, ...
Frankly, I think it's obvious that ...

question: Which phrase is used to CHALLENGE somebody else's opinion?
choices: You're entitled to your opinion, but ...
It's a good idea in principle, but ...

question:
Read this passage and answer the question:

Hyenas are big, ugly and dangerous animals and they are the most common large predator in Africa. They often survive on eating animals that are already dead, but they will also attack and eat live animals – including cows, sheep, goats and even people.

According to the passage, hyenas survive on:
choices: only live animals like cows and sheep
eating animals that are already dead, but also attack live animals
fruits and plants found in the wild
only animals that humans provide for them

question:
Read this passage and answer the question:

In the past, the animals often attacked people living in the city, so the people found a way to deal with them: they cut holes in the city walls and threw food to the hyenas. They realised that the hyenas were no longer hungry because of this, and attacked people less often.

How did the people of Harar solve the problem of hyena attacks?
choices: They built higher walls around the city.
They moved the hyenas to a distant area.
They cut holes in the walls and threw food to the hyenas.
They trained the hyenas to be friendly.
